refined_fr.refined_fr: Ensure the teleoperation link availability meets or exceeds the customer-defined target to maintain continuous remote control capability.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; refining clarifies the focus on availability performance as a measurable attribute critical for teleoperation effectiveness and safety.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: Maintain continuous network connectivity for teleoperation commands and telemetry.
Detect and recover from link failures within acceptable time limits.

candidate_dps: Redundant communication channels with automatic failover
Robust error detection and correction protocols
Adaptive bandwidth management and link quality monitoring
selected_dp.selected_dp: Redundant communication channels with automatic failover

potential_sources: Interaction with safety alert systems during failover
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates link availability concerns and supports modular failover mechanisms, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of multiple communication channels
Risk of increased complexity leading to integration challenges
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating error correction protocols in subsequent decomposition levels to complement redundancy.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing teleoperation communication links and ensuring link availability.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outgoing data stream conveying vehicle status and link health.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Incoming teleoperation commands requiring reliable delivery.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope; contains teleoperation but with high diffusion due to multiple responsibilities.
Physical vehicle module has limited direct control over link availability.
Primary module for teleoperation link management; best containment and low diffusion.
Supports safety alerts but not directly responsible for link availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning link availability DP to Teleoperation module centralizes responsibility and minimizes diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with NGHE Solution for overall system coordination.
Risk of overloading Teleoperation module with additional safety-related link monitoring.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor interface complexity and consider splitting link monitoring and failover logic if diffusion increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with a clear logical solution and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Possible complexity in Teleoperation module if additional link management features are added.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment for the DP with minimal diffusion to other modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring link availability metrics through Teleoperation interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine sub-FRs to include error correction and adaptive bandwidth management in next decomposition level.
Review interface definitions to ensure no overlap or ambiguity with Safety Services.

decision.justification: While the decomposition is sound, further refinement of sub-FRs and inclusion of complementary DPs is needed to fully address link availability robustness.
next_level_sub_frs: Maintain continuous network connectivity for teleoperation commands and telemetry.
Detect and recover from link failures within acceptable time limits.

revise.why: To incorporate additional functional aspects such as error correction and adaptive management, and to select complementary DPs accordingly.

assumptions: Multiple communication channels are available for redundancy.
Teleoperation module can handle additional link management responsibilities.
open_risks: Integration complexity with other system modules.
evidence_needed: Performance data on link availability with redundancy implemented.
Interface load and complexity metrics for Teleoperation module.
